subject: Gaming
title: Columbine and the Blame Game
description: A fast breakdown of Columbine and the Blame Game for builders.

hook.text: After Columbine in 1999, media blamed Doom. The claim that violent games cause real violence dominated headlines.

buildup.text: Politicians and media outlets pointed to the shooters' gaming habits as a causal explanation.

discovery.text: Researchers studied the link for decades. The American Psychological Association found no causal connection.

twist.text: Countries with higher gaming rates, like Japan and South Korea, have far lower violent crime rates.

climax.text: The Supreme Court ruled in 2011 that video games are protected speech. The blame game lost in court.

punchline.text: Decades of blame. No causal link found. The Supreme Court agreed.

quiz.question: What did the Supreme Court rule about video games in 2011?
options: Video games are protected speech under the First Amendment
Only adults can buy any video game
